metadata.title: OpenAI Raw Model Response
metadata.purpose: Raw OpenAI Responses API result captured before harness output validation.

output_text: {"metadata":{"document_id":"project_manager_report.json","title":"Project Manager Report","source_format":"json","document_authority":"generated_artifact"},"report_status":"needs_clarification","report_summary":"The supplied task text is too under-specified to determine a bounded transformation beyond generic trajectory review. Repo-local invariant and operational sources are present and validated, but the task authority 'test' does not identify an intended surface, probe, or admissibility question. Current posture remains scaffold-to-runtime transition work focused on context/compiler/model-validation readiness, with the dominant tension still the gap between compiled pre-call artifacts and runtime-evidenced PM route completion.","report_source_coverage":{"static_context_packet":{"consumed":true,"basis":["Used project_spec, governance_primitives, open_decisions, active_implementation_plan, and active_implementation_tracker to determine project thesis, current implementation posture, active seams, acceptance probes, and approval boundaries.","Used source_coverage entries to confirm the static authority packet was included and Pydantic-validated with no missing sources."]},"task":{"consumed":true,"basis":["Used task text 'test' as the current task authority.","Determined the task text is ambiguous and does not name a target surface, proof obligation, or requested admissibility decision."]}},"trajectory_review":{"current_posture":"The repository is in an active compiler-and-contract hardening slice: static context compilation, agent context compilation, repo snapshot compilation, provider payload rendering, model selection, and raw OpenAI call capture are implemented and tested, while the package CLI plan workflow and validated Project Manager runtime-output save path are not yet runtime-proven.","thesis_attractor":"Realize the bounded CLI harness workflow where a plan command compiles live repo-local context, routes through the Project Manager contract, validates the structured PM report, saves inspectable run artifacts, and stops before implementation or source mutation.","drift_detection":{"drift_detected":true,"description":"There is repo-local evidence of implementation drift between the project spec's highest-value workflow and current runtime posture: the spec centers on `python -m harness plan ...` saving a validated PM report, while `harness/__main__.py` still fails honestly and current runtime evidence only proves pre-call assembly, payload rendering, model-selection, and raw-response capture as separate surfaces."},"invariant_constraints":["Project Manager remains read-only and cannot authorize mutation, shell execution, worker invocation, or ambient repo inspection beyond provided runtime inputs.","Runtime capability claims require saved runtime evidence and deterministic probes; scaffold-only or separated pre-call/post-call surfaces are not sufficient proof of the intended workflow.","The harness must preserve explicit separation between invariant authority, task authority, role contracts, compiled runtime inputs, runtime outputs schemas, and saved runtime artifacts.","Templates/examples must not be treated as live runtime inputs; runtime truth must come from live .json operational documents and compiled packets.","The plan workflow must stop before implementation or repository source mutation.","Report validation must enforce cross-field coherence, including report_status versus proof_frontier.blocked behavior."],"task_constraints":["The current task authority is only the string 'test'.","The task does not specify whether the request is to review trajectory, validate a seam, assess admissibility, inspect drift, or target a particular runtime surface.","No approval-boundary crossing was requested.","No repo-local spec amendment was requested."],"structural_tension":"The project already has substantial contract and compiler scaffolding plus tests for pre-call and provider-adjacent surfaces, but the user task is too ambiguous to select which admissible proof step should be evaluated or recommended inside that larger gap from scaffolded/runtime-partial state to validated end-to-end PM plan-route evidence."},"proof_frontier":{"constraint_conflicts":[],"dominant_tension_justification":"The limiting factor for trustworthy next-step guidance in this run is not missing invariant authority; it is ambiguous task authority. The static packet is complete enough to identify the project-level frontier, but the task text does not say which admissibility judgment is being requested against that frontier.","blocked":true,"blocking_reason":"Task authority is too ambiguous to determine a bounded next admissible transformation for this run.","missing_basis":["Clarification of what 'test' is meant to test: trajectory review, schema/validator behavior, context compilation, provider payload rendering, raw OpenAI call capture, or the intended end-to-end PM plan route.","Clarification of the target surface or acceptance probe the user wants evaluated.","Clarification of whether the user wants a generic project-status assessment or a judgment about a specific implementation seam."],"next_admissible_transformation":null,"affected_surfaces":["project_manager_report.json output semantics for this run","task authority interpretation","current implementation seam selection"],"non_affected_surfaces":["global governance primitives","Project Manager read-only boundary","worker adapters","implementation/file-mutation workflow","provider abstraction beyond the first OpenAI path"],"stop_conditions":["Stop until the task authority names a concrete evaluation target or admissibility question.","Stop if a requested next step would require changing global governance, role permissions, or crossing into implementation/mutation without explicit approval.","Stop if any recommendation would claim end-to-end runtime proof not substantiated by the acceptance probes and saved artifacts named in project_spec."]}}

basis: Called OpenAI Responses API using rendered provider payload.
